hero_section.title: Julian Dismute
hero_section.subtitle: Christian Speaker & Minister [cite: 82]
hero_section.tagline: Calling this generation to live with conviction, follow Jesus without compromise, and lead with courageous obedience. [cite: 83]
hero_section.quote: Faith is not meant to be comfortable. It is meant to transform you. [cite: 84]

who_he_is.heading: Identity [cite: 197]
who_he_is.description: Julian Dismute is a Christian speaker and minister committed to proclaiming the gospel of Jesus Christ and equipping believers to live with conviction in a culture of compromise. [cite: 198] Through preaching, teaching, and digital ministry, he challenges students, athletes, leaders, and churches to move beyond passive belief and into active, disciplined obedience. [cite: 199]
who_he_is.core_message: Following Jesus is not casual - it requires surrender, conviction, and courage. [cite: 200]

points: To call this generation to bold, uncompromising faith in Jesus Christ. [cite: 202]

intro.description: Julian Dismute is a Christian speaker, minister, and digital communicator committed to proclaiming the gospel of Jesus Christ and equipping believers to live with conviction and courageous obedience. [cite: 2] Shaped by adversity and sustained by the faithfulness of God, Julian speaks with clarity, authenticity, and a deep passion to see lives transformed by the power of Jesus. [cite: 3] He does not speak to entertain he speaks to awaken. [cite: 4]

adversity_to_calling.heading: From Adversity to Calling [cite: 5]
adversity_to_calling.story: Julian's story began with significant hardship. After losing both of his parents at a young age, he was forced to navigate life and identity through seasons of pain, uncertainty, and struggle. [cite: 6] Sports became an important outlet and identity during those years, shaping his discipline, resilience, and leadership. [cite: 7] In 2016, a defining encounter with Jesus Christ transformed Julian's life and clarified his calling. [cite: 9] What once felt like loss became purpose, and what once felt like confusion became direction. [cite: 10]

ministry_and_leadership.heading: Ministry & Leadership [cite: 12]
ministry_and_leadership.details: Julian currently serves with the Fellowship of Christian Athletes (FCA), where he works closely with coaches and athletes, mentoring students, leading Bible studies, and helping young leaders live out their faith with courage and conviction. [cite: 13] He regularly preaches at his local church and is invited to speak at churches, youth camps, conferences, and special events across the country. [cite: 14] Whether speaking to a congregation, a group of student-athletes, or a leadership gathering, Julian's message remains consistent: Faith must be lived with conviction, not just professed. [cite: 15]

family_and_life.heading: Family & Life [cite: 19]
family_and_life.details: Julian has been married to his wife for 13 years, and together they are raising their son Kyrie in West Des Moines, Iowa. [cite: 20] Outside of ministry, Julian enjoys spending time with his family, watching his son play basketball, golfing, and connecting with friends. [cite: 21]

intro.tagline: Equipping believers and leaders to live with conviction and lead with courage. [cite: 143]
intro.description: Julian partners with pastors and ministry leaders to strengthen believers, challenge complacency, and call the Church to courageous obedience to Jesus Christ. [cite: 144] His teaching is rooted in Scripture, structured with clarity, and focused on building conviction that lasts beyond the moment. [cite: 147]

topics.title: Courageous Obedience [cite: 151]
topics.description: Julian confronts the gap between belief and behavior, challenging audiences to move beyond verbal agreement and into disciplined, daily obedience to Christ. [cite: 153]

topics.title: Conviction in a Compromising Culture [cite: 157]
topics.description: This message addresses identity, moral courage, and leadership integrity, helping audiences stand firm without retreat or arrogance. [cite: 159]

topics.title: From Comfort to Calling [cite: 161]
topics.description: Julian calls believers back to seriousness, unity, and wholehearted devotion to Christ helping audiences rediscover purpose and live with renewed urgency. [cite: 164]

intro.heading: A Movement for Conviction and Revival [cite: 59]
intro.description: Blaze Collective exists to awaken bold faith and call believers to wholehearted obedience to Jesus Christ. [cite: 60] In a culture where faith is often reduced to comfort, this ministry exists to ignite conviction, [cite: 61] strengthen believers, and cultivate environments where people take their walk with God seriously. [cite: 62]

blaze_collective.heading: Blaze Collective [cite: 64]
blaze_collective.description: Blaze Collective is a grassroots community of believers united by a hunger for deeper faith. [cite: 65]

wildfire.heading: Wildfire [cite: 70]
wildfire.description: Wildfire is a revival gathering hosted by Blaze Collective. [cite: 71] What began in 2024 as a step of obedience has grown into a space where people can encounter God in a real and personal way. [cite: 72] These nights are centered on worship, the preaching of the Word, and a call to deeper faith. [cite: 75] No hype. No performance. Just hunger for God and a call to obedience. [cite: 76] People don't just attend. They respond. [cite: 77] This is not routine church. [cite: 78] This is revival. [cite: 79]

intro.heading: See the Message. Experience the Impact. [cite: 111]
intro.description: Julian Dismute communicates with clarity, conviction, and purpose - delivering messages that challenge, strengthen, and call people to live out their faith with courage. [cite: 112]

intro.heading: INVITE JULIAN - SPEAKING REQUEST [cite: 26, 27]
intro.description: Thank you for considering Julian for your event. Please share a few details below so we can learn more about your vision and how we can best serve your community. [cite: 28]

about_you: Your Role (Pastor, Leader, Coach, Event Organizer, etc.) [cite: 32]
about_event: Type of Event (Sunday Service, Youth Night/Camp, Conference, Leadership Event, School Assembly, Athletic/FCA Event, Other) [cite: 37]
Who will be in the room? (Students, adults, leaders, athletes, etc.) [cite: 40]
heart_of_event: What is your heart or goal for this event? [cite: 42]
What do you feel your people need most right now? [cite: 43]
logistics_and_budget: Speaking Focus (Spiritual growth / deeper faith, Leadership & conviction, Identity & purpose, Overcoming adversity, Athlete/team culture, Open/led as needed) [cite: 45]
Will travel be required? If yes, nearest airport [cite: 47]
Will travel and lodging be provided? [cite: 48]
Do you have a budget range in mind? (Under $1,000, $1,000-$2,500, $2,500-$5,000, $5,000+, Not sure) [cite: 50]

Booking.submission_message: Thank you for reaching out. We take every invitation seriously and prayerfully consider each opportunity. [cite: 56] Our team will follow up with you soon. [cite: 57]
